UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores core, non-authentication related information about a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address. Used for display and identification, not for authentication credentials.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

UserSettings.title: UserSettings
UserSettings.description: Stores user-specific preferences and application settings.

userId.description: Unique identifier for the UserSettings entity, also serving as a reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 UserSettings)

darkModeEnabled.description: Indicates if dark mode is enabled for the user's interface.

notificationPreferences.description: A JSON string storing various notification preferences (e.g., email alerts, in-app notifications).

language.description: The preferred language for the user interface (e.g., 'en-US', 'es-MX').

UploadedFile.title: UploadedFile
UploadedFile.description: Stores metadata about files (screenshots, images) uploaded by users for analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UploadedFile entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who uploaded the file. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UploadedFile)

analysisId.description: Reference to the Analysis entity that this file is associated with. Can be null if uploaded but not yet analyzed. (Relationship: Analysis 1:N UploadedFile)

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

fileType.description: The MIME type of the uploaded file (e.g., 'image/png', 'application/pdf').

storagePath.description: The path or key to the file in Firebase Storage.

downloadUrl.description: The public download URL for the file from Firebase Storage.

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the file was uploaded.

extractedText.description: Text content extracted from the file, if applicable (e.g., OCR from an image).

Analysis.title: Analysis
Analysis.description: Represents a single instance of a financial analysis performed based on user input.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Analysis entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated the analysis. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Analysis)

title.description: A concise title summarizing the analysis results.

summary.description: A high-level summary of the findings and potential impact.

estimatedMonthlySavings.description: The total estimated monthly savings identified in this analysis.

analysisDate.description: Timestamp when the analysis was performed.

status.description: The current status of the analysis (e.g., 'completed', 'pending', 'failed').

inputContent.description: The raw text content provided by the user if the input method was 'pasted_text' or 'manual_notes'.

uploadedFileIds.description: An array of references to UploadedFile entities associated with this analysis. (Relationship: Analysis N:N UploadedFile)

detectedItemIds.description: An array of references to DetectedItem entities generated by this analysis. (Relationship: Analysis 1:N DetectedItem)

beforeComparison.description: A JSON string representing the financial situation before applying recommendations.

afterComparison.description: A JSON string representing the projection of the financial situation after applying recommendations.

DetectedItem.title: DetectedItem
DetectedItem.description: Represents a specific financial finding or opportunity identified within an analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DetectedItem entity.

analysisId.description: Reference to the Analysis entity this item belongs to. (Relationship: Analysis 1:N DetectedItem)

title.description: A concise title for the detected item (e.g., 'Netflix Subscription', 'Duplicate Utility Charge').

summary.description: A detailed explanation of the detected item and its implications.

category.description: The classification of the detected item (e.g., 'Subscription', 'Duplicate Charge', 'Hidden Fee', 'Trial Ending', 'Price Increase', 'Unusual Spending').

confidenceLevel.description: A confidence score (0-100) indicating the certainty of the detection.

urgencyLevel.description: An urgency score (0-100) indicating how quickly action should be taken.

itemSavingsEstimate.description: The estimated monthly savings associated with resolving this specific detected item.

currentAmount.description: The current cost associated with this detected item before optimization.

optimizedAmount.description: The projected cost associated with this detected item after optimization.

recommendedActionIds.description: An array of references to RecommendedAction entities for this detected item. (Relationship: DetectedItem 1:N RecommendedAction)

copyableMessage.description: A pre-written, copyable message relevant to this finding (e.g., a cancellation script).

status.description: The current status of this detected item (e.g., 'active', 'resolved', 'ignored').

relatedDate.description: A relevant date for the item, e.g., trial ending date, renewal date.

Subscription.title: Subscription
Subscription.description: Represents a recurring charge or subscription, either detected by the system or manually added/confirmed by the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subscription entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this subscription. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Subscription)

name.description: The common name of the subscription (e.g., 'Netflix Premium', 'Spotify Family Plan').

provider.description: The service provider or vendor of the subscription.

monthlyCost.description: The equivalent monthly cost of the subscription, regardless of billing cycle.

nextRenewalDate.description: The next date the subscription is scheduled for renewal or billing.

startDate.description: The date when the subscription originally started.

status.description: The current status of the subscription (e.g., 'active', 'cancelled', 'on-hold', 'trial').

detectedItemId.description: Reference to the DetectedItem if this subscription was initially identified by an analysis. Can be null if manually added or not directly linked to a specific detection. (Relationship: DetectedItem 1:1 Subscription)

notes.description: User-added personal notes or details about the subscription.

Alert.title: Alert
Alert.description: Represents an urgent notification or critical insight generated for the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Alert entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile for whom the alert is generated. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Alert)

detectedItemId.description: Reference to the DetectedItem that triggered this alert, if applicable. (Relationship: DetectedItem 1:N Alert)

title.description: A concise title for the alert (e.g., 'Trial Ending Soon!', 'Unusual Spending Detected').

message.description: The detailed message content of the alert, explaining the situation and potential impact.

type.description: The category or type of alert (e.g., 'urgent', 'warning', 'info', 'insight').

severity.description: A numerical value (0-100) indicating the severity or importance of the alert.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the alert was created.

isDismissed.description: Boolean indicating if the user has dismissed this alert.

dismissedAt.description: Timestamp when the alert was dismissed, if applicable.

RecommendedAction.title: RecommendedAction
RecommendedAction.description: Represents a specific action recommended to the user to address a detected financial item.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RecommendedAction entity.

detectedItemId.description: Reference to the DetectedItem this action is recommended for. (Relationship: DetectedItem 1:N RecommendedAction)

title.description: A concise title for the action (e.g., 'Cancel Netflix Subscription', 'Negotiate internet bill').

description.description: Detailed instructions or context for carrying out the recommended action.

associatedMessage.description: A copyable message or script directly associated with performing this action (e.g., email template).

estimatedImpact.description: The estimated financial impact (e.g., savings) from successfully completing this action.

status.description: The current status of the action (e.g., 'pending', 'completed', 'skipped', 'in_progress').

dueDate.description: An optional due date or target date for completing the action, especially for reminders.

definition.description: Stores core profile information for a user. The document ID matches the user's Firebase Authentication UID. Access is restricted to the owning user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching request.auth.uid.

definition.description: Stores user-specific preferences and application settings. Each user has a single document, typically with a fixed ID (e.g., 'default'). Includes denormalized 'userId' (already in schema) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching request.auth.uid.

definition.description: Stores metadata for files uploaded by a user. Includes denormalized 'userId' (already in schema) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who uploaded the file.

params.name: fileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the uploaded file metadata document.

definition.description: Stores individual financial analysis reports generated for a user. Includes denormalized 'userId' (already in schema) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who initiated the analysis.

params.name: analysisId
params.description: The unique identifier for the analysis report.

definition.description: Represents a specific financial finding within an analysis. **Must include denormalized 'userId' and 'analysisId' fields** in the document for authorization independence, allowing rules to check ownership without `get()` calls to parent documents.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent analysis.

params.name: analysisId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent analysis.

params.name: detectedItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the detected item.

definition.description: Stores details about recurring charges or subscriptions owned by a user. Includes denormalized 'userId' (already in schema) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this subscription.

params.name: subscriptionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the subscription.

definition.description: Stores urgent notifications or insights generated for a user. Includes denormalized 'userId' (already in schema) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user for whom the alert is generated.

params.name: alertId
params.description: The unique identifier for the alert.

definition.description: Stores specific actions recommended for a detected item. **Must include denormalized 'userId', 'analysisId', and 'detectedItemId' fields** in the document for authorization independence, allowing rules to check ownership without `get()` calls to parent documents.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent analysis.

params.name: analysisId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent analysis.

params.name: detectedItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent detected item.

params.name: actionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the recommended action.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access patterns by adhering strictly to the core design principles. The primary strategy employed is **Path-Based Ownership** combined with **Denormalization for Authorization Independence**.

**Authorization Independence:**
All user-owned data is nested under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection. For root collections like `users`, `uploaded_files`, `analyses`, `subscriptions`, and `alerts`, the `userId` field is explicitly stored within each document and also present in the document's path. This allows security rules to check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` or `request.auth.uid == userId` (from the path wildcard) directly, without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent documents. This prevents issues with atomic operations (transactions/batches) and simplifies rule debugging.

For nested subcollections, specifically `detected_items` and `recommended_actions`, the original schemas only linked to their immediate parent (`analysisId` for `DetectedItem`, `detectedItemId` for `RecommendedAction`). To ensure full authorization independence as mandated, documents within these subcollections **MUST be denormalized to include the `userId` and all relevant parent IDs (`analysisId`, `detectedItemId`) directly within their document data**. For example, a `DetectedItem` document at `/users/{userId}/analyses/{analysisId}/detected_items/{detectedItemId}` will contain `userId`, `analysisId`, and `detectedItemId` fields in its payload. This allows security rules to evaluate access for any document in isolation, using only `request.auth.uid` against the denormalized `userId` field in the document itself, without costly and complex `get()` calls to `Analysis` or `User` documents.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The hierarchical structure directly supports efficient and secure `list` operations. By placing all user-specific data under `/users/{userId}/...`, any query initiated by an authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) is inherently scoped to their own data. For instance, a query on `/users/myUserId/analyses` will only return analyses belonging to `myUserId`. The security rule `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;` for top-level user subcollections, or `allow list: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId;` for denormalized subcollections, is sufficient. Firestore's security engine will automatically apply this path-based filter before returning results, ensuring that users can only list data they own. This prevents accidental data exposure and ensures that rules act as gatekeepers rather than post-query filters, which is crucial for performance and security.
